name: Arch
description: The first archaerhodopsin-based voltage indicator, establishing that microbial opsins can optically report membrane potential, though with extremely dim baseline fluorescence.

spectrum.name: Arch
spectrum.note: Digitized directly from Kralj 2012 main-text Fig. 1b — the real wild-type Arch spectrum at pH 7.3 (the fluorescent, Schiff-base-protonated state): solid black absorption (blue edge ~0.65 at 460 nm, slight ~480 nm trough, α-band peak at 558 nm, falling to baseline by ~650 nm) and dashed black emission (peak ~687 nm, tail to ~776 nm; only the 626–776 nm portion is drawn in the figure). The pH 11.1 (green) deprotonated, non-fluorescent state was excluded. Each array normalized to max = 1.0. Peaks match Table 1 (λabs 558 nm, λem 687 nm). (Supersedes an earlier curve that had been digitized from the Arch(D95N) mutant panel in Suppl. Fig. 1a and blue-shifted to WT peaks; Fig. 1b gives the WT curve directly with no shifting needed.)

voltage.name: Arch
voltage.note: Digitized from the Fluorescence(A.U.)–voltage curve in Maclaurin 2013 Fig. 2A (Arch WT; ~1.0 at −150 mV to ~1.35 at +150 mV), converted to %ΔF/F normalized to −70 mV = 0. Roughly linear, ~35% over the full ±150 mV range (≈11% for the −70→+30 step). This is shallower than the 40–45%/100 mV headline from Kralj/Hochbaum (measured under higher illumination) — Arch's apparent sensitivity rises with illumination intensity.

kinetics.note: Arch (wild-type) has a sub-millisecond response; Kralj reports 'sub-millisecond temporal resolution' and did not separately resolve on/off at their time resolution (stored as 0.5/0.5 ms). NOTE: the 41 ms slow component sometimes quoted for 'Arch' belongs to the Arch(D95N) variant, which D95N slowed the response to — not WT. See the Hochbaum Table S2 entry for resolved on/off.

Arch(WT), HEK293, resolved bi-exponential fit (Hochbaum Suppl. Table 2): τup = 0.6 ms (mono-exponential, on); τdown τ1 0.25 ms (67%)/τ2 1.9 ms → off = 0.67×0.25 + 0.33×1.9 = 0.8 ms. Confirms Arch WT's sub-millisecond kinetics.

dynamicRangeData.note: ΔF/F per 100 mV, Kralj 2012 (high-intensity 1P widefield imaging). Kralj's own text describes only a 'roughly linear twofold increase between −150 mV and +150 mV' (Fig. 1c) — an explicit '45% per 100 mV' is not printed in this paper; 45% is the commonly-cited near-operating-range value for wild-type Arch (cf. Hochbaum's 40% per 100 mV entry). Arch's apparent sensitivity is strongly illumination-dependent and varies widely across papers (~11–45%/100 mV); the F-V curve (Maclaurin 2013 Fig. 2A, digitized here) is shallower (~35% over the full −150→+150 mV range, ≈11% for the −70→+30 step) than this high-illumination headline value.

ΔF/F per 100 mV, wild-type Arch. Hochbaum 2014 main text (alongside the Fig. 1c F–V curve) states verbatim: 'the sensitivity of QuasAr2 was substantially improved over those of both Arch (40% per 100 mV) and Arch(D95N) (60% per 100 mV)'; the sensitivities were quantified over the −70→+30 mV step region under high-intensity 1P imaging.

brightnessData.note: Computed from EC=48900 M⁻¹cm⁻¹ at 559 nm × QY≈1.1×10⁻⁴ (Herasymenko 2025) = 5.38 M⁻¹cm⁻¹. Reference EGFP EC×QY = 56000 × 0.60 = 33600 → 5.38/33600 ≈ 0.00016.

apWidthData.sample: Cultured rat hippocampal neurons, in vitro
apWidthData.note: Derived by digitizing the averaged optical single-AP fluorescence waveform (red trace, 269 events averaged) in the inset of Kralj 2012 Fig. 2f, recorded in cultured rat hippocampal neurons at a 2-kHz frame rate. Time calibrated from the panel's 4-ms scale bar (≈12.5 px/ms); FWHM = full width of the red curve above the half-baseline-to-peak level ≈ 4.3 ms (overlay-verified). No FWHM value is printed in the paper; this is a plot-derived optical-AP width. The width is dominated by the AP shape and Arch's kinetics rather than the frame period (τ_on/off sub-ms ≪ FWHM; 0.5-ms frame period ≪ 4.3 ms). All Kralj experiments were at 24 °C.
